task_completion.description: Navigate to or open the configuration for the 'payment-gateway' deployment in the 'ecommerce' namespace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the Rancher UI logged in and viewing the 'payment-gateway' deployment in the 'ecommerce' namespace, or a terminal showing a command like 'kubectl edit deployment payment-gateway -n ecommerce'.

task_completion.description: Replace static environment variables with Downward API fieldRefs for APP_POD_NAME, APP_POD_NAMESPACE, HOST_IP, and POD_IP in the 'audit-logger' container.
task_completion.visual_evidence: YAML editor or Rancher UI showing the 'audit-logger' container's environment variables configured with 'valueFrom: fieldRef: fieldPath:' pointing to 'metadata.name', 'metadata.namespace', 'status.hostIP', and 'status.podIP' respectively.

task_completion.description: Replace MAIN_CPU_LIMIT with a resourceFieldRef pointing to the 'gateway-app' container's CPU limits.
task_completion.visual_evidence: YAML editor or Rancher UI showing the MAIN_CPU_LIMIT environment variable in the 'audit-logger' container configured with 'valueFrom: resourceFieldRef:' where 'containerName' is 'gateway-app', 'resource' is 'limits.cpu', and 'divisor' is '1m'.

task_completion.description: Add a downwardAPI volume named 'pod-metadata' mapping labels and annotations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: YAML editor or Rancher UI showing a new volume under 'volumes' named 'pod-metadata' of type 'downwardAPI'. It should contain 'items' mapping 'fieldRef: fieldPath: metadata.labels' to path 'labels' and 'fieldRef: fieldPath: metadata.annotations' to path 'annotations'.

task_completion.description: Mount the 'pod-metadata' volume in the 'audit-logger' container at '/etc/podinfo'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: YAML editor or Rancher UI showing a 'volumeMounts' entry in the 'audit-logger' container with 'name: pod-metadata' and 'mountPath: /etc/podinfo'.

integrity.description: The agent must actually apply and save the changes to the cluster.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the agent clicking 'Save' in the Rancher UI, a 'Deployment updated' success notification, or successfully saving and exiting a 'kubectl edit' terminal session.

integrity.description: The modifications must be made to the actual 'payment-gateway' deployment in the 'ecommerce' namespace.
integrity.visual_evidence: UI breadcrumbs, URL, or terminal commands confirming the edits are being applied to 'payment-gateway' in the 'ecommerce' namespace, rather than a similarly named mock file.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine Rancher UI or a real Kubernetes terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of the Rancher web interface (browser address bar showing https://localhost) or a standard terminal emulator running kubectl, not a standalone local text editor with a disconnected YAML file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target namespace: 'ecommerce'. Target deployment: 'payment-gateway'. The task requires modifying the 'audit-logger' container to use Downward API fieldRefs for APP_POD_NAME (metadata.name), APP_POD_NAMESPACE (metadata.namespace), HOST_IP (status.hostIP), and POD_IP (status.podIP). It also requires a resourceFieldRef for MAIN_CPU_LIMIT pointing to 'gateway-app' limits.cpu with divisor '1m'. A downwardAPI volume named 'pod-metadata' must be created mapping metadata.labels to 'labels' and metadata.annotations to 'annotations', and this volume must be mounted at /etc/podinfo in the 'audit-logger' container.
